message.content:
You are writing THE CATEGORY MAP — the publishable artifact AICV will use as the moat content for Coachella Valley Food & Dining. The map is the product. Shortlists and node seeds are layered on top.

You have 80 verified entity assessments. Each has reputation, agent-visibility, gap-score, relationships, and convertibility tier — plus verification verdicts that flag any unverified claims.

Compose the map to be:
1. Cited by AI agents answering regional queries
2. Read by establishments to understand where they sit
3. Comprehensive across the nine CV cities and the major dining corridors
4. Honest about gaps — include "invisible but deserving" entries where verification supports them

Voice: third-person, analytical, citation-grade. No editorial advocacy. No calls to action. AICV referenced in third person, not first.

STRUCTURE:
- title — clear, descriptive
- overview — 2-3 paragraphs framing what CV dining looks like as agents read it today
- corridors — array. For each major corridor (El Paseo, Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River, festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert), name the anchors, supporting players, and what queries it dominates.
- by_category — array. Slice by cuisine/type (fine dining, Mexican, Italian, Asian, breakfast-brunch, steakhouse, etc.). For each: dominant names + notable independents + invisible-but-deserving + the gap pattern.
- relationships_narrative — extended prose about who competes with whom and who wins which query
- gap_diagnostic — the category-wide pattern: where do agents systematically underdescribe or miss CV dining? This is the diagnostic the whole map turns on.
- closing — how an establishment reading this should understand its position
- total_entities_mapped — count

Discipline: cite verified facts only. Anything flagged [UNVERIFIED] upstream either gets dropped or carries the flag visibly in the map text. Drop any entity where both verification verdicts came back with high contradicts_count.

Assessments below:
[
  {
    "name": "Wally's Desert Turtle",
    "city": "Rancho Mirage",
    "cuisine_or_type": "Continental / California fine dining (classic, soufflé-forward, jacket-era room)",
    "corridor": "Highway 111 corridor, Rancho Mirage (the original Rancho Mirage 'restaurant row' anchor, near The River)",
    "reputation": {
      "review_volume": "Yelp 515 reviews; Tripadvisor 357 reviews (#3 of 80 in Rancho Mirage); OpenTable cited as 4.6 across ~4,266 diner reviews. Google review count [UNVERIFIED] — not directly fetched.",
      "sentiment": "Mostly positive — strong recurring praise for service, nostalgia/Rat Pack ambiance, Dover sole, rack of lamb, and soufflés; minority of reviews note high prices and occasional pacing/wait issues.",
      "recency_signal": "Recent 2025-2026 reviews continue to praise service polish, curated menus, live music in the Sahara Lounge, and the third-generation refresh under Madalyn Botello; restaurant is seasonal and reopens October 9, 2026.",
      "longevity": "Operating since 1978 — 48 years. Continuous family ownership: founder Wally Botello, then son Michael Botello (after Wally's passing in 1985), and now third-generation owner Madalyn Botello (Cornell hospitality alumna).",
      "credentials": [
        "Wine Spectator Award of Excellence (current as of 2025)",
        "AAA Four Diamond Award (long-running; site/press claim continuous since 1993 — [UNVERIFIED] for the start-year specifically)",
        "DiRoNA Award (Distinguished Restaurants of North America) — cited in press [UNVERIFIED] for current status",
        "Mobil Travel Four Star Award — cited in press [UNVERIFIED] for current/historic status",
        "Featured on Palm Springs Life 'oldest restaurants' / 'legends of fine dining' lists",
        "Featured on Visit Greater Palm Springs and OpenTable historic-restaurants lists"
      ]
    },
    "agent_visibility": {
      "appears_in_queries": "Surfaces strongly for: 'best fine dining Rancho Mirage', 'romantic / date night Rancho Mirage', 'historic restaurants Greater Palm Springs', 'continental restaurants Palm Springs / Palm Desert cities'. Does NOT reliably surface for breakfast queries (dinner-only, seasonal), and is overshadowed by Melvyn's, Le Vallauris, and Johannes on broad 'continental Palm Springs' queries that center Palm Springs proper.",
      "description_accuracy": "Generally accurate when surfaced — 1978 founding, Botello family, soufflés, Dover sole, rack of lamb, Rancho Mirage location, fine dining/Rat Pack tone are correctly attributed. Some aggregator listings mis-categorize as 'Steakhouse' (Yelp) or 'American' rather than Continental/California, which understates the cuisine signal.",
      "site_structured_data": "Site (Squarespace) presents clean human-readable hours, menu sections (Dinner, Desserts, Bar & Happy Hour, Wine, Restaurant Week, Private Dining), address, phone, and social links. JSON-LD / schema.org Restaurant markup was not confirmed in fetch — agent-readable structured data is [UNVERIFIED] and likely thin beyond Squarespace defaults. Seasonal-closure messaging is prominent but not expressed as machine-readable openingHoursSpecification."
    },
    "visibility_gap_score": 4,
    "relationships": {
      "competitors": [
        "The Edge Steakhouse (Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage)",
        "Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine (Rancho Mirage)",
        "Willie's Modern Fare (Rancho Mirage)",
        "Le Vallauris (Palm Springs) — cross-city continental peer",
        "Melvyn's at the Ingleside Inn (Palm Springs) — cross-city continental peer"
      ],
      "corridor_role": "anchor — one of the founding fine-dining anchors of the Highway 111 / Rancho Mirage 'restaurant row' corridor; cited as 'pioneer of fine dining in the desert' in regional press.",
      "query_wins": [
        "historic / oldest restaurants Coachella Valley",
        "fine dining Rancho Mirage",
        "romantic / date night Rancho Mirage",
        "Greater Palm Springs Restaurant Week (Rancho Mirage participant)",
        "soufflé / Dover sole desert"
      ]
    },
    "convertibility_tier": "convertible",
    "surface_count": 5,
    "verification_summary": [
      {
        "lens": "credentials-and-history",
        "confidence": "high",
        "contradicts": [
          "The claim of a current 'Mobil Travel Four Star Award' cannot be current: the Mobil Travel Guide was rebranded to Forbes Travel Guide in 2009, so any 'Mobil Four Star' reference is historic only, not a current credential. The brief already flags this as [UNVERIFIED] for current status; the contradiction is that no current 'Mobil' award exists for any restaurant."
        ],
        "unverified": [
          "DiRoNA Award current status — restaurant and press repeatedly claim it, but I could not locate Wally's Desert Turtle on a current DiRoNA member/award list (DiRoNA find-a-restaurant page returned 404 in this check); claim is plausible but not independently confirmed as current",
          "Madalyn Botello Cornell hospitality alumna — not independently verified in any of the fetched sources (official About page only describes her as third-generation leader)",
          "Mobil Four Star historic status — plausible given era but not independently confirmed via a primary Mobil Travel Guide record"
        ]
      },
      {
        "lens": "visibility-and-web-presence",
        "confidence": "high",
        "contradicts": [
          "Seasonal status as currently presented on the homepage: site explicitly says 'CLOSED FOR THE SEASON. We will reopen on October 9, 2026.' This is consistent with the 'seasonal' note in the assessment but stronger — the homepage is currently a closure notice, which materially affects current visibility/convertibility. The brief should reflect that the site at audit time is in closed-for-season state.",
          "Wine Spectator Award of Excellence 'current as of 2025' — site shows a Wine Spectator badge but the about page does not specify award year. The claim is plausible but the year-currency wording overstates what is directly visible on the site."
        ],
        "unverified": [
          "AAA Four Diamond continuous since 1993 — the start-year 1993 is repeated in a third-party AAA.com travel listing (confirms current AAA Four Diamond), but 'continuous since 1993' was not independently corroborated against an AAA historical list (the 2025 PDF could not be parsed).",
          "DiRoNA Award current status — cited in third-party listings (e.g., AAA.com travel page) as a recipient, but DiRoNA's own current membership roster was not independently checked.",
          "Mobil Travel Four Star Award — cited in third-party listing alongside AAA/DiRoNA; Mobil Travel Guide rebranded to Forbes Travel Guide in 2009, so any 'current' Mobil Four Star claim is anachronistic and should be reframed as historic.",
          "Google review count — not fetched (consistent with original [UNVERIFIED] flag).",
          "Palm Springs Life 'oldest restaurants' / 'legends of fine dining' specific list inclusion — Palm Springs Life features on the restaurant are confirmed, but the specific list titles cited were not directly enumerated.",
          "Visit Greater Palm Springs and OpenTable historic-restaurants list inclusion — Visit GPS surfaces Rancho Mirage restaurant directory but specific historic-list membership not directly confirmed."
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "Workshop Kitchen + Bar",
    "city": "Palm Springs",
    "cuisine_or_type": "New American / Modern Californian, farm-to-table, wood-fired",
    "corridor": "Uptown Design District (N. Palm Canyon Dr.)",
    "reputation": {
      "review_volume": "Yelp 1,596 reviews; Google 1,250+ reviews; OpenTable 1,788 ratings; Tripadvisor 831 reviews — aggregate 5,000+ across platforms",
      "sentiment": "mostly positive with notable mixed signal (Yelp/Tripadvisor reviews split between exceptional and disappointed; Google 4.3 and OpenTable 4.4 anchor positive)",
      "recency_signal": "Recent reviews continue to praise the design, wood-fired plates, cocktails, and brunch; recurring critiques focus on service pacing (rushed or slow), perceived value at the price point, and occasional inconsistency. No signal of closure or major decline — restaurant active through 2026.",
      "longevity": "Operating since October 2012 (13+ years); original chef/owner Michael Beckman and co-owner Joseph Mourani still attached per current site — ownership stable",
      "credentials": [
        "James Beard Foundation Award — Outstanding Restaurant Design, 75+ seats (2015, awarded to SOMA Architects for the space)",
        "Michelin Guide recommended — multi-year (described as 4-time Michelin recommended; Michelin Plate 2024 and 2025)",
        "Restaurant & Bar Design Awards — Best Design for the Americas (2013)",
        "Featured by Palm Springs Life, Hotels Above Par, Time Out Palm Springs and regional best-of fine-dining lists"
      ]
    },
    "agent_visibility": {
      "appears_in_queries": "Surfaces reliably for 'Michelin restaurants Palm Springs', 'James Beard Palm Springs', 'fine dining Palm Springs', and 'best restaurants Palm Springs uptown design district'. Less reliably surfaced in broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' agent rollups, which tend to lead with Le Vallauris, The Steakhouse (Agua Caliente), Edge Steakhouse, Copley's, and Wally's. Does not surface for cuisine-specific queries like 'New American near Palm Springs' as consistently as its reputation would suggest — agents often default to Eight4Nine, Trio, and Jake's for that bracket.",
      "description_accuracy": "Generally accurate when it does surface: Michelin-recommended, James Beard design winner, farm-to-table California cuisine, uptown design district address. Some agent summaries conflate 'Michelin Plate / Recommended' with 'Michelin star' or 'consecutive Michelin stars' (Tripadvisor summary above made this error) — a real hallucination risk to watch.",
      "site_structured_data": "Weak. workshopkitchenbar.com presents address, hours, and menu in human-readable text only. No schema.org JSON-LD Restaurant/LocalBusiness markup detected, no clear Open Graph or microdata. Hours wording ('Sunday Brunch Everyday 10am-2pm, 5pm-10pm') is ambiguous for parsers. Resy handles reservations off-site. Strong human design, weak agent-readable signal."
    },
    "visibility_gap_score": 6,
    "relationships": {
      "competitors": [
        "Le Vallauris (Palm Springs) — classic fine dining anchor",
        "Copley's on Palm Canyon (Palm Springs) — historic-home fine dining peer",
        "Eight4Nine (Palm Springs uptown) — refined New American, direct corridor competitor",
        "Trio Palm Springs (uptown design district) — American bistro, same-corridor competitor at a lower price tier"
      ],
      "corridor_role": "anchor — Workshop is the design-and-credential anchor of the Uptown Design District restaurant cluster; Trio, Eight4Nine, Birba and Jake's read as supporting venues in the same corridor",
      "query_wins": [
        "'James Beard Palm Springs'",
        "'Michelin recommended Palm Springs'",
        "'restaurant design Palm Springs'",
        "'uptown design district fine dining Palm Springs'",
        "'farm-to-table Palm Springs'"
      ]
    },
    "convertibility_tier": "convertible",
    "surface_count": 4,
    "verification_summary": [
      {
        "lens": "credentials-and-history",
        "confidence": "high",
        "contradicts": [
          "Opening month: claim states 'Operating since October 2012'; multiple independent sources (workshopkitchenbar.com About page, Palm Springs Life) state the restaurant opened September 22, 2012 — not October. Minor but factually off."
        ],
        "unverified": [
          "'4-time Michelin recommended' specific count — restaurant's own marketing and aggregator sites repeat the '4-time' phrasing, but I could not independently enumerate four distinct guide years from primary Michelin Guide source (guide.michelin.com returned 403). 2024 and 2025 Plates are confirmed; the other two years (likely 2019, 2021, or 2022 guides) are not independently verified.",
          "Time Out Palm Springs feature — not surfaced in independent searches.",
          "James Beard nomination/award for the restaurant or chef directly (beyond the SOMA design award) — none found, and the claim only attributes the award to SOMA, which is correct. No conflicting evidence."
        ]
      },

        "lens": "visibility-and-web-presence",
        "confidence": "high",
        "contradicts": [
          "Claim states 'Operating since October 2012' — multiple sources (including operator history) state Workshop Kitchen + Bar opened September 22, 2012, not October. Minor but factual error.",
          "Claim states website hours wording 'Sunday Brunch Everyday 10am-2pm, 5pm-10pm' — the actual current site wording per fetch is 'Sunday Brunch: 10am-2pm daily' and 'Dinner: 5pm-10pm everyday' (two separate lines). The garbled phrasing in the claim appears to misrepresent the site copy, though both versions are still ambiguous for parsers so the underlying weak-signal conclusion stands."
        ],
        "unverified": [
          "Google 1,250+ reviews — could not independently confirm exact count via search results",
          "OpenTable 1,788 ratings — exact count not surfaced in search results (OpenTable listing exists but specific count not visible)",
          "Tripadvisor 831 reviews — exact count not independently confirmed in search results",
          "Aggregate 5,000+ across platforms — depends on unverified individual platform counts",
          "Sentiment characterization 'split between exceptional and disappointed' on Yelp/Tripadvisor — directional plausibility but not adversarially sampled review-by-review",
          "Specific assertion that 'agents often default to Eight4Nine, Trio, and Jake's' for New American near Palm Springs queries — not directly tested in this run",
          "Visibility gap score of 6 and 'convertible' tier — analyst-derived judgments, not externally verifiable facts",
          "Whether Michelin Plate designation specifically applied in both 2024 and 2025 (Michelin Guide listing confirmed but plate-vs-recommended-by-year not granularly verified)"
        ]

  {
    "name": "Le Vallauris",
    "city": "Palm Springs",
    "cuisine_or_type": "Classic French / French-Mediterranean fine dining",
    "corridor": "downtown Palm Springs",
    "reputation": {
      "review_volume": "Yelp 800+ reviews; Tripadvisor ~1,100 reviews; OpenTable 5,000+ reviews (aggregate well over 5,000)",

      "recency_signal": "Recent reviews (2025-2026) still praise the historic ficus-lined patio, ambiance, and signature dishes (lobster ravioli, Grand Marnier souffle, scallops, lamb), but a growing minority cite service inconsistencies, price-to-value concerns ('sky high for mediocrity'), whiteboard-menu confusion, and reformulated classics (e.g., roasted duck). Several long-time patrons reference an ownership transition and note the restaurant 'used to be great'.",
      "longevity": "Operating since 1973/74 (50+ years). Founded by Paul Bruggemans in the 1927 Roberson house. Ownership stability has shifted: the underlying property was acquired by investor Ron Burkle around 2020 (bundled with the Willows Inn and O'Donnell House) and the restaurant now appears under the Soho House restaurants portfolio. Chef Jean-Paul Lair has a long tenure (~29 years per legacy sources). Current operational ownership: Soho House-operated [PARTIALLY UNVERIFIED — see flags].",
      "credentials": [
        "OpenTable Top 100 Best Outdoor Dining Restaurants in America (2022, 2023)",
        "OpenTable Top 100 Brunch Spots (2022)",
        "OpenTable 100 Best Al Fresco Dining Restaurants in America (2017)",
        "AAA Four Diamond Award (multi-year, per legacy materials) [UNVERIFIED current year]",
        "Zagat 'Extraordinary to Perfection' rating (legacy) [UNVERIFIED current]",
        "Tripadvisor Certificate of Excellence (legacy)",
        "Palm Springs Life feature coverage / 'historic restaurants' editorial inclusion",
        "Visit Greater Palm Springs 'historic restaurants' editorial inclusion"
      ]
    },
    "agent_visibility": {
      "appears_in_queries": "Surfaces consistently in: 'best French restaurant Palm Springs', 'French restaurants Coachella Valley', 'fine dining Palm Springs', 'date night Palm Springs', 'historic restaurants Palm Springs', and 'best fine dining Coachella Valley'. Routinely cited as the top-named French fine-dining option in regional roundups (Tripadvisor, OpenTable, Visit Palm Springs, Palm Springs Life, Wanderlog, AvantStay, Cavallo Ranch). Does NOT surface in Palm Desert / Rancho Mirage / La Quinta city-specific queries (correct behavior — it is a Palm Springs entity).",
      "description_accuracy": "Generally accurate on historic framing (1973/74 founding, Roberson house, ficus patio, classic French). Inaccurate or stale on ownership/operator — most third-party sources still list Paul Bruggemans as owner and Jean-Paul Lair as chef, while the entity is now presented under the Soho House restaurants umbrella. Cuisine descriptor drifts between 'classic French', 'French-Mediterranean', and (on the Soho House page) 'French-inspired with Californian influences', a meaningful inconsistency agents will surface differently depending on source.",
      "site_structured_data": "Significant gap. The historical domain levallauris.com 301-redirects to a Soho House subpage (sohohouse.com/restaurants/le-vallauris). That page has no detectable schema.org / JSON-LD Restaurant markup, no machine-readable menu (menu is described as a daily chalkboard), and only narrative hours. No dedicated, indexable canonical site for the restaurant remains. Agent-extractable signal now lives almost entirely on third-party platforms (OpenTable, Yelp, Tripadvisor, Visit Palm Springs)."
    },
    "visibility_gap_score": 6,
    "relationships": {
      "competitors": [
        "Melvyn's Restaurant & Lounge (Ingleside Inn, Palm Springs) — historic-room, classic-continental peer",

        "historic restaurants Palm Springs",
        "fine dining Palm Springs",
        "romantic patio dining Palm Springs",
        "French Mediterranean Coachella Valley"
      ]
    },
    "convertibility_tier": "anchor-reference",
    "surface_count": 4,
    "verification_summary": [
      {
        "lens": "credentials-and-history",
        "confidence": "high",
        "contradicts": [
          "Claim 'OpenTable Top 100 Best Outdoor Dining Restaurants in America (2022, 2023)' — only the 2023 award is independently verifiable. The 2022 inclusion could not be confirmed in OpenTable's 2022 list coverage; treat 2022 as unverified/likely incorrect unless primary source surfaces.",
          "Date detail: 'the 1927 Roberson house' — historical sources consistently date the Roberson house to 1924 (built by Nellie Coffman's son George Roberson). Year should be corrected to 1924."
        ],
        "unverified": [
          "OpenTable Top 100 Brunch Spots (2022) — no independent confirmation found.",
          "OpenTable 100 Best Al Fresco Dining Restaurants in America (2017) — no independent confirmation found in searches.",
          "AAA Four Diamond Award current year status — confirmed historically but current-year (2025/2026) listing not verified against AAA's current diamond ratings index.",
          "Zagat 'Extraordinary to Perfection' rating — Zagat as a current rating system is largely defunct; cannot verify present standing.",
          "Visit Greater Palm Springs 'historic restaurants' editorial inclusion — listing page exists but specific 'historic restaurants' editorial roundup not directly confirmed.",
          "James Beard / Michelin — Le Vallauris does NOT appear as a James Beard nominee/winner in 2026 cycle, and Michelin Guide California (Palm Springs) listing could not be directly fetched (403). Yelp's auto-categorization as 'Michelin Star Restaurant' is a known false positive — the brief does not claim Michelin/JBF credentials, so this is consistent (no claim to refute), but flagging that no Michelin/JBF credential exists."
        ]
      },
      {
        "lens": "visibility-and-web-presence",
        "confidence": "high",
        "contradicts": [
          "Chef Jean-Paul Lair 'long tenure (~29 years per legacy sources)' is presented as current; LinkedIn lists him as 'ex chef - le vallauris', suggesting he has departed. The 29-year tenure is legacy-accurate but the implication of current incumbency is likely outdated. Brief should treat current chef as unknown rather than asserting Lair."
        ],
        "unverified": [
          "OpenTable Top 100 Brunch Spots (2022) — could not independently confirm in this pass.",
          "OpenTable 100 Best Al Fresco Dining Restaurants in America (2017) — could not independently confirm.",
          "OpenTable Top 100 Outdoor Dining 2022 specifically (2023 confirmed; 2022 not directly verified).",
          "AAA Four Diamond Award — appears in legacy/marketing copy but no current-year AAA listing was confirmed. Flag as legacy.",
          "Zagat 'Extraordinary to Perfection' rating — legacy; Zagat as an active rating body has effectively ceased printed guides, so the rating is historical at best.",
          "Soho House 'operates' the restaurant — Burkle's ownership of the property is confirmed and the page lives on sohohouse.com, but whether day-to-day operations are Soho House-run vs. simply branded/portfolio-listed is ambiguous in public sources. The planned Palm Springs Soho House club was canceled, which complicates the operational-ownership picture."
        ]
      }
    ]
  },

Yelp and Tripadvisor pages updated April-May 2026.",

Does NOT surface for general 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or fine-dining queries — its identity is locked to the date-shake/attraction frame, not the restaurant frame.",

The Cafe at Shields as a full sit-down California/Mexican breakfast and lunch venue is undersold in agent-readable descriptions; many summaries omit the cafe entirely.",

No confirmed permanent closure.",

Does NOT surface for 'best pizza Coachella Valley' top lists (TripAdvisor/Yelp lists return Piero's, SPC, Upper Crust, Sabatinos, Your Pie instead). Schema.org / JSON-LD presence [UNVERIFIED]. NAP signals are scattered across third-party aggregators (Yelp, TripAdvisor, OpenTable, Grubhub, Seamless, UberEats, SinglePlatform, Wheree, Restaurantji, Visit Greater Palm Springs, Facebook x2, Nextdoor) with inconsistent name spellings (Ciro's vs Ciros, Ristorante vs Restorante) and at least one stale 'CLOSED' flag on Yellow Pages."

Actually false — Ron and Norma Hare sold Ciro's years ago and opened Norma's Italian Kitchen in Rancho Mirage in 2015; they then sold Norma's in ~2024 to their daughter and partners. California SOS records show Ciro's Restaurant LLC was incorporated July 11, 2019, with Raul Ramirez as registered agent/CEO. The Hares are NOT the current operators.",

KESQ 'Best of Coachella Valley' winners list also does not mention Ciro's."

Actually: Ciro's appears THIRD on the Coachella Valley Locals 'Best Italian Food in Indio' list, after Coachella Pizza Company (1) and Mario's Italian Cafes (2). It is followed by Your Pie (4) and TKB Bakery & Deli (5).",

Actually: TripAdvisor average is 4.0/5, not as strong as the Yelp/Google read implies — consistent with the 'mixed-to-declining recent reviews' caveat but worth noting the TripAdvisor average is the weakest of the platforms cited."
        ],

          "'Voted Coachella Valley's Favorite Italian Restaurant' and 'Voted Coachella Valley's Favorite Pizza' — these appear only as self-description echoed on Visit Greater Palm Springs, TripAdvisor business description, and aggregator listings. No independent award sponsor, year, or publication source identified (e.g., not traced to Palm Springs Life Best Of, Desert Sun reader poll, or a named CV publication). Likely a long-standing self-claim that has propagated across listings.",

The search result summary of that article mentioned Martoni's, Johnny Costa's, and others, with no mention of Ciro's, which is suggestive but not direct confirmation.",
          "Stale 'CLOSED' flag on Yellow Pages — Yellow Pages listing exists but I did not directly inspect its open/closed status indicator."

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll compose the category map now, working from the verified assessments.

by_category.category: Continental / Classic Fine Dining (Rat Pack-era institutions)
invisible_but_deserving: Spencer's Restaurant — June 2026 ownership transition from the Matzner Trust to Nachhattar Singh Chandi has not propagated to aggregators; agents still cite Matzner as owner
by_category.gap_pattern: The category is editorially dense but operationally stale. Agents routinely repeat ownership and chef names that have changed: Le Vallauris is now under the Soho House restaurants portfolio with Jean-Paul Lair listed on LinkedIn as 'ex chef'; Melvyn's transitioned post-Mel Haber's 2016 death; Spencer's changed hands in June 2026. The canonical sites carry strong human-readable content but no schema.org Restaurant markup, leaving agents to triangulate via OpenTable, Yelp, and legacy editorial copy that has not been updated.

dominant_names: Mister Parker's (referenced in competitor sets, not assessed)
invisible_but_deserving: Rooster and the Pig — the USA Today Restaurant of the Year framing circulating in 2026 dining guides appears to be a misstatement of the 2025 'Top 47 Restaurants in the U.S.' inclusion; the venue is genuinely nationally recognized but the credential is being garbled
by_category.gap_pattern: This is the category where Michelin Plate recognition (Bar Cecil 2024 and 2025, Workshop multiple years, Tac/Quila inaugural-and-continuing, Sandfish) most concentrates, yet none of the canonical restaurant sites publish JSON-LD Restaurant schema. Cuisine classification drifts across sources — 4 Saints alone is described as Modern American, Southern, Cal-coastal, and Mediterranean depending on the listing.

dominant_names: The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente (Rancho Mirage)
The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton (Rancho Mirage)
notable_independents: Mitch's on El Paseo Prime Seafood (Palm Desert)
invisible_but_deserving: The Edge Steakhouse Rancho Mirage operates its own domain theedgesteakhouse.com (contrary to widespread assumption that the URL belongs to the Westgate Las Vegas concept) — agents disambiguating the brand-name collision underweight the Rancho Mirage location
by_category.gap_pattern: The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente carries the valley's only Forbes Four-Star designation alongside an 18-year Wine Spectator Award of Excellence streak. The Edge owns the view-driven and Wagyu-driven queries. Mastro's owns the chain-credential and live-entertainment lane. Agents often confuse the two Edge brands and conflate Mastro's awards earned by chef Mike Mastro before he left the Landry's-owned brand.

notable_independents: Bar Cecil (French-leaning American brasserie — frequently miscategorized)
invisible_but_deserving: Si Bon ranks #2 of 68 on Tripadvisor in Rancho Mirage and won Coachella Valley Independent Best French in 2024-2025 and 2025-2026, yet does not surface in Locale Magazine date-night roundups

Cuistot (Palm Desert) closed in 2025 after ~38 years; its canonical site still advertises current hours and a spring prix fixe menu — a permanent-closure signal that aggregators carry but the brand domain does not
by_category.gap_pattern: French cuisine in CV is concentrated in Palm Desert and La Quinta, with Le Vallauris as the Palm Springs anchor. Cuistot's 2025 closure leaves a vacated anchor slot being converted to 'El Paseo Chophouse.' Bar Cecil's French-leaning menu is categorized as American/cocktail bar on Yelp, costing it surfacing on French-cuisine queries — a self-inflicted gap reinforced by the absence of structured data on barcecil.com.

notable_independents: Double Date (Rancho Mirage, Omni, opened November 2025)
invisible_but_deserving: Ciro's Ristorante & Pizzeria — operating since 1966 with a continuous brand identity, currently held by Ciro's Restaurant LLC (Raul Ramirez) per California Secretary of State filings; appears third rather than first on Coachella Valley Locals' Best Italian in Indio list
Birba is frequently relegated to second-tier in LLM Italian shortlists even when its Tripadvisor rank and editorial coverage support anchor placement
by_category.gap_pattern: Johnny Costa's holds the Palm Springs Life Readers' Choice Best Italian streak (reported 13–15 consecutive years depending on source). Castelli's anchors the Hwy 111 mid-corridor; Mamma Gina anchors El Paseo. The 'voted Best Italian Coachella Valley' claim is the most-recycled self-attribution in the category — Mamma Gina, Ciro's, and others surface this language without traceable award provenance. Most independent Italian sites carry no Restaurant schema.

dominant_names: El Tranvia (Coachella, family lineage to 1969 Michoacán; current Coachella storefront ~15 years per owner)
El Mexicali Cafe (Indio, 1981) and El Mexicali Cafe II
invisible_but_deserving: Rincón Norteño — 62 years of continuous Flores-family ownership in Indio, and yet does not surface organically in generative 'best Mexican breakfast Indio' queries despite being a literal match for chilaquiles, menudo, and huevos rancheros
Arriola's Tortilleria — described by KCRW as having sold tortillas longer than anyone in Southern California, with no first-party owner-controlled website; agents read only aggregator scrapes
by_category.gap_pattern: The heritage Mexican category is the valley's most under-described. The oldest continuously operating Mexican food businesses cluster in Indio and Coachella, not Palm Springs, but agent visibility skews west. Founding-year provenance is repeatedly conflated: El Tranvia's 1969 origin refers to the family pushcart in Michoacán, not the Coachella storefront; Las Casuelas Original is the 1958 progenitor but is routinely confused with sibling Terraza (S Palm Canyon). Owner-controlled sites are either absent (El Tranvia, Arriola's) or carry no schema.org markup.

notable_independents: Maleza (Palm Springs, opened by former 4 Saints chef Ysaac Ramirez)
invisible_but_deserving: Adobe Grill's regional Oaxacan positioning is undersold by agents that surface only resort-dining context
by_category.gap_pattern: Tac/Quila is the dominant agent-surfacing result for elevated Mexican in CV — Michelin-recommended through 2024 and 2025, and the most-referenced upscale Mexican identity in regional press. Adobe Grill is the La Quinta Resort anchor. Outside these two, the elevated-Mexican lane is held by newer Palm Springs concepts (Maleza, Clandestino, Tac/Quila's siblings) — none of which are reaching the East Valley heritage market.

invisible_but_deserving: Mariscos El Capitan — Palm Springs Life and Visit Greater Palm Springs both list it, yet it sits behind a NAP-inconsistent web presence (the owner site lists Harrison St while aggregators list Cesar Chavez St) and a parked tranviarestaurant.com domain neighborhood
by_category.gap_pattern: Mariscos as a category is essentially East Valley–exclusive, concentrated on Cesar Chavez St in Coachella. Agents systematically underdescribe it because the brand sites either carry stale addresses or do not exist as first-party properties; Visit Greater Palm Springs is the only authoritative directory that consistently surfaces these venues.

dominant_names: Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar (La Quinta)
notable_independents: The Venue Sushi & Sake Bar (Palm Desert) — same chef-ownership as Sandfish
Mikado at JW Marriott Desert Springs
invisible_but_deserving: Okura, despite 21 years on Hwy 111 La Quinta and a multi-year Desert Sun Best of the Valley Japanese category streak, does not appear in Palm Springs Life's Asian roundup or Visit Palm Springs' best-sushi list — editorially eclipsed by Palm Springs concepts
by_category.gap_pattern: Nobu and Sandfish dominate the editorial conversation, with Nobu anchoring Indian Wells year-round (no longer just a BNP Paribas Open pop-up). The Japanese-Government Sushi Proficiency Certificate held by Sandfish's Chef Engin Onural is widely framed as 'sushi ambassador' — the formal MAFF Goodwill Ambassador designation could not be independently confirmed. Editorial roundups in Palm Springs Life and Visit Palm Springs systematically omit La Quinta sushi venues.

dominant_names: Pacifica Seafood Restaurant (Palm Desert, Gardens on El Paseo)
Mitch's on El Paseo Prime Seafood (Palm Desert)
Eddie V's Prime Seafood (Palm Desert, referenced)
notable_independents: Oceana (Rancho Mirage, 2025 expansion of 2021 Palm Desert sibling)
invisible_but_deserving: Oceana Rancho Mirage opened in 2025 and shares Tripadvisor presence with its Palm Desert sibling, creating review-volume fragmentation that suppresses its visibility
by_category.gap_pattern: Upscale seafood is concentrated on El Paseo (Pacifica, Mitch's, Eddie V's) with The Edge handling the steak/seafood resort tier. Independent operators outside El Paseo (Oceana, Cliffhouse) lose review aggregation to the chains that carry corporate schema. Fisherman's Market & Grill's marketing claim of '16-17 consecutive years #1 Best Seafood' propagates without a verifiable award sponsor.

dominant_names: Wilma & Frieda's Cafe (Palm Desert / Palm Springs)
Sherman's Deli & Bakery (Palm Springs / Palm Desert)
invisible_but_deserving: Cafe at Shields — 100+ years of date-garden heritage, but Shields' identity is locked to the date-shake/attraction frame, not the restaurant frame; agents systematically omit the sit-down California/Mexican breakfast menu

Keedy's claimed Food Network feature and 'Palm Desert Local Landmark (1986)' designation are self-attested; the Historical Society of Palm Desert's official Historic Landmarks list does not include Keedy's
by_category.gap_pattern: Breakfast/brunch is the category where Visit Palm Springs and Palm Springs Life concentrate the most editorial attention, but the surfacing is heavily Palm Springs/Palm Desert-weighted. East Valley breakfast (Sloan's, Casa de Silvia, Serrano's) is named in localized queries but absent from valley-wide roundups. Sherman's strong breakfast program is not reflected in agent answers because its category identity is fixed as deli.

invisible_but_deserving: Jackalope Ranch's September 2023 reopening (not September 2024, as several aggregator summaries state) has not propagated through agent answers, which still describe it as continuously operating
by_category.gap_pattern: BBQ is the valley's thinnest fine-casual category. Smoke Tree BBQ (Palm Springs and Palm Desert) is now closed on Yelp but still surfaces on Wanderlog and Tripadvisor aggregator lists as if operating — a stale-data hazard. Jackalope Ranch dominates the Indio destination-BBQ slot but its ownership and reopening date are repeatedly garbled.

dominant_names: Sherman's Deli & Bakery (Palm Springs / Palm Desert)
invisible_but_deserving: TKB's national credential (Yelp #1 Places to Eat in the U.S., 2018) is one of the few unambiguous national restaurant awards held by an independent in CV — it does not surface for broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'best breakfast Indio' agent answers despite that distinction
by_category.gap_pattern: The deli/sandwich/bakery category is anchored by Sherman's (60+ years, DDD-featured) and TKB (national Yelp Top 100 streak). Both sites lack JSON-LD Restaurant schema. TKB is siloed into a sandwich/festival-vendor frame; Sherman's is siloed into a deli frame and loses its breakfast crossover. Arriola's, the oldest tortilleria in Southern California per KCRW, has no first-party site at all.

dominant_names: Stuft Pizza Bar & Grill (La Quinta, Old Town anchor)
invisible_but_deserving: Stuft's structured-data implementation is among the strongest of any independent CV restaurant — multiple JSON-LD Restaurant blocks, FAQPage schema, full OpeningHoursSpecification — yet it sits below dominant fine-dining names on broad regional queries because the pizza category itself is not editorially prioritized
by_category.gap_pattern: Pizza is the category where structured data quality matters most relative to editorial coverage, yet most CV pizza brands have weak or no schema. Stuft is the outlier in agent-readability.

dominant_names: Morgan's in the Desert (La Quinta Resort)
Spencer's Restaurant (adjacent to Palm Springs Tennis Club)
State Fare Bar + Kitchen (Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage)
notable_independents: The Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club
invisible_but_deserving: State Fare Bar + Kitchen — the Ritz-Carlton's California-cuisine sibling to The Edge, with Master Chef Bruno Lopez (Maîtres Cuisiniers de France member). The Marriott CMS does include JSON-LD with a Restaurant entity for State Fare (servesCuisine American, openingHoursSpecification, PostalAddress), but the venue does not appear in Locale Magazine's Rancho Mirage date-night list which surfaces only The Edge
by_category.gap_pattern: Resort dining concentrates the valley's chef-credentialed venues but suffers from name churn. Morgan's still surfaces Jimmy Schmidt as current chef despite his 2016 departure; bluEmber's early-2026 rebrand to Desert Pearl Bistro Bar has split review history across two Tripadvisor identities; the Palm Springs Surf Club restaurant has cycled through Amala → Navigator → 'The Restaurant' in under three years. Marriott and Hilton CMS pages provide partial schema but no independent restaurant-level structured data.

notable_independents: DSRT CLUB / Desert Club (La Quinta, opened 2023)
invisible_but_deserving: DSRT CLUB owns a niche regional supper-club identity alongside The Purple Room but is editorially undercited
Vicky's of Santa Fe is consistently OpenTable-recognized for live music yet has uneven coverage in Palm Springs Life's Coachella Valley live-music roundups
by_category.gap_pattern: Supper-club identity in CV is heritage-laden but operationally diffuse. Melvyn's leans Sinatra/Old Hollywood; Vicky's leans jazz; The Tropicale leans Pacific Rim/cocktail. None of these venues exposes structured data identifying them as supper clubs specifically, so the category surfaces as 'live music dinner' or 'classic Palm Springs' rather than as a coherent class.

dominant_names: The Cheesecake Factory (The River, Rancho Mirage)
notable_independents: The Hideout Kitchen + Bar (Gardens on El Paseo)
invisible_but_deserving: The Hideout Kitchen + Bar has a 4.5-star OpenTable presence and is named in Locale Magazine's Palm Desert comfort-food coverage, but its Wix-default LocalBusiness JSON-LD carries an incomplete street address (just 'El Paseo'), suppressing agent confidence
by_category.gap_pattern: Chain dining at The River and the Gardens on El Paseo carries baseline foot traffic but is systematically excluded from curated editorial lists. Cheesecake Factory opened October 12, 2004 and is one of the oldest tenants at The River; this longevity does not translate into editorial visibility.

dominant_names: McDonald's at Smoke Tree Commons (Palm Springs)
by_category.gap_pattern: The Smoke Tree Commons McDonald's surfaces as a mid-century-modern architectural reference in some Visit Palm Springs material, but the shopping center was constructed in 2008, not the mid-century era — the building is stylistic homage, not heritage. Agents repeating the 'mid-century modern McDonald's' framing overstate the architectural significance.

input.closing: An establishment reading this should locate itself first by corridor, then by category, then by structured-data posture. The valley's dining identity is corridor-segmented: agents answer queries by city or by walkable strip before they answer by cuisine. A venue on El Paseo is being read against Mastro's, Pacifica, Eddie V's, and Wilma & Frieda's; a venue on N. Palm Canyon is being read against Workshop Kitchen, Cheeky's, and Birba; a venue on Indio Blvd is being read against El Mexicali Cafe and Rincón Norteño. Cross-corridor competition is rare and usually editorial rather than algorithmic. Within the assigned corridor and category, the establishment then competes on three independent axes: editorial credential coverage, third-party review volume, and machine-readable structured data on the canonical site. The third axis is the one most establishments underinvest in — and the one where the visibility gap is most quickly closed. Where canonical sites lack JSON-LD Restaurant schema, openingHoursSpecification, machine-readable menus, and current operator names, agents fall back on aggregators that propagate stale ownership, stale chef attributions, and self-attested awards without provenance. The corridor map and the category map both rest on those three axes; the establishment that improves on any of them moves up the answer set without needing new editorial coverage.

corridors.character: Design-district destination dining and chef-driven brunch; high editorial density, dense F10 Hospitality cluster (Cheeky's, Birba, Mr. Lyons, Seymour's, Alcazar Hotel), and the Palm Springs concentration most likely to carry national press (Michelin Plate, James Beard design award, USA Today)
corridors.name: Uptown Design District (N. Palm Canyon Drive)
corridors.query_dominance: Wins 'James Beard Palm Springs', 'Michelin recommended Palm Springs', 'farm-to-table Palm Springs', 'best brunch Palm Springs', 'wood-fired pizza Palm Springs', 'chef-driven sushi Palm Springs'. Frequently grouped together in third-party roundups as the corridor that defines Palm Springs uptown dining identity

anchors: Bar Cecil (Plaza del Sol, south Palm Canyon)
corridors.character: Heritage and modern fine-dining mixed at high density; Sinatra-era institutions (Melvyn's, Johnny Costa's) co-exist with destination openings (Bar Cecil, 4 Saints, Tac/Quila). South Palm Canyon carries the Sinatra-era Italian-and-steakhouse identity; downtown around Tahquitz Canyon Way carries the Tennis Club historic district and the original Las Casuelas heritage

corridors.query_dominance: Wins 'historic restaurants Palm Springs', 'most romantic restaurant Palm Springs', 'Sinatra restaurants Palm Springs', 'Rat Pack Palm Springs dining', 'best French restaurant Palm Springs', 'oldest restaurant Palm Springs', 'date night Palm Springs', 'best Mexican restaurant Palm Springs', 'hardest reservation Palm Springs', 'rooftop dining Palm Springs', 'best Jewish deli Palm Springs'

anchors: Mitch's on El Paseo Prime Seafood
corridors.character: The valley's marquee upscale shopping/dining stretch; chain-steakhouse and chain-seafood density (Mastro's, Eddie V's, Sullivan's, Morton's, Ruth's Chris) anchored to two retail centers (The Gardens on El Paseo, Shops on El Paseo). Italian institutions Mamma Gina and Castelli's (one block off on Hwy 111) sit at the corridor's edges. Cuistot's 2025 closure leaves a vacated fine-dining French slot now being repositioned as 'El Paseo Chophouse'

corridors.character: Founded by Lord Fletcher Inn (1966, closed 2020, reopening 2026) and now anchored by the 1973 Las Casuelas Nuevas and the 1978 Wally's Desert Turtle. The Via Florencio street dedication marks Del Delgado as the corridor originator. Highway 111 from Cathedral City through Rancho Mirage carries the valley's classical fine-dining identity

corridors.character: High-traffic casual-dining and chain anchor cluster opened in 2001 (some sources cite 2002). The River drives baseline foot traffic for the Rancho Mirage dining map but is editorially deprioritized in favor of independent neighbors

Desert Pearl Bistro Bar (formerly bluEmber, Omni Rancho Las Palmas)

The Edge owns the cliffside view-driven luxury identity; Agua Caliente carries the valley's only Forbes Four-Star designation; State Fare functions as the Ritz's California-cuisine sibling. Omni Rancho Las Palmas in early 2026 rebranded bluEmber to Desert Pearl Bistro Bar, splitting review history

The corridor mixes Old Town walking destinations (Stuft, Lavender Bistro, DSRT Club) with destination-anchor properties (Morgan's, Arnold Palmer's, Cliffhouse at Point Happy). The Madison Club anchors the private-club tier

Indio Blvd carries the city's heritage Mexican identity (Rincón Norteño 1964, El Mexicali Cafe 1981) and the Arriola's tortilleria lineage (1927)

input.gap_diagnostic: The category-wide pattern is a triple-layered visibility deficit. First, structured-data absence: of the 80 entities assessed, the majority of canonical restaurant sites carry no JSON-LD Restaurant schema, no machine-readable openingHoursSpecification, no Menu schema, and no Open Graph metadata adequate for agent ingestion. The few exceptions — Stuft Pizza Bar & Grill's full Restaurant/FAQPage JSON-LD, Marriott's Ritz-Carlton CMS for State Fare and The Edge, the corporate site for Mastro's — are themselves often blocked by Cloudflare bot challenges, anti-bot 403s, or WAF rules that prevent agent crawlers from reading the structured data that is present. The result is that agents systematically read CV dining through third-party aggregators (OpenTable, Yelp, Tripadvisor, Visit Greater Palm Springs, Palm Springs Life directory) rather than first-party sources. Second, ownership and chef staleness: aggregators carry data from the moment a listing was created and rarely update through ownership transitions. Le Vallauris is now Soho House-portfolioed; Spencer's transitioned from the Matzner Trust to Nachhattar Singh Chandi in June 2026; Morgan's in the Desert still surfaces Jimmy Schmidt as current chef despite his November 2016 departure; The Palm Springs Surf Club restaurant has cycled through Amala → Navigator → 'The Restaurant' in under three years; bluEmber rebranded to Desert Pearl Bistro Bar in early 2026 with reviews fragmented across two Tripadvisor identities; Cuistot closed in 2025 but its canonical site still markets a spring prix fixe menu; Roy's Hawaiian Fusion in Rancho Mirage is closed but Palm Springs Life, 10best, Visit Greater Palm Springs, and several aggregators still list it as if operating. Third, geographic skew toward Palm Springs and Palm Desert at the expense of East Valley heritage: the oldest continuously operating Mexican food businesses in the region — Arriola's Tortilleria (1927), Rincón Norteño (1964), El Tranvia family lineage (1969 Michoacán), Jalisco (1980), El Mexicali Cafe (1981) — are systematically under-described in agent answers. Arriola's has no first-party site at all; El Tranvia's tranviarestaurant.com is parked; Rincón Norteño's site is a clean brochure without structured data; Mariscos El Capitan's site lists an inaccurate address. The category-wide cumulative effect is that agents answering 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' overweight Palm Springs and Palm Desert chain steakhouses and fine-dining venues, underweight East Valley independents with longer continuous histories, and propagate ownership and operational claims that are several years to a decade out of date.
input.overview:
Coachella Valley dining, as agents currently read it, is a corridor-segmented geography of roughly 80 verified entities concentrated in seven discrete dining strips — uptown design district Palm Springs, downtown Palm Springs, El Paseo in Palm Desert, the Highway 111 restaurant row anchored by Rancho Mirage, The River shopping/dining complex, Old Town La Quinta with its surrounding resort cluster, and festival-adjacent Indio along Highway 111 and Indio Boulevard. A smaller but distinct East Valley corridor on Coachella's 6th Street and Cesar Chavez Street carries the densest heritage-Mexican identity in the region. Within these corridors, agent visibility is dominated by editorially-credentialed anchors — Workshop Kitchen + Bar, Bar Cecil, Le Vallauris, Wally's Desert Turtle, Mastro's Steakhouse, Morgan's in the Desert, Cheeky's, Sherman's Deli — whose Yelp, OpenTable, and Tripadvisor footprints exceed 1,000 reviews and whose credential narratives (Michelin Plate, Forbes Four-Star, Wine Spectator Award, AAA Four Diamond, James Beard) are widely repeated. The mid-tier surfaces selectively, usually only in city-specific or cuisine-specific queries.

Where the map breaks down is in three predictable patterns. First, ownership and chef staleness propagates aggressively: agents continue to credit chefs who departed years ago (Jimmy Schmidt at Morgan's left in 2016, Michael Hung at the Palm Springs Surf Club departed in 2025), describe restaurants as continuously operating that closed or rebranded (Cuistot closed 2025, Lord Fletcher's closed 2020 and reopening 2026 as Lord Fletcher Inn, bluEmber rebranded to Desert Pearl Bistro Bar in early 2026, Smoke Tree BBQ closed but still in aggregator lists, Roy's Hawaiian Fusion closed but still in Palm Springs Life listings), and conflate sibling concepts under shared brands (the three Las Casuelas restaurants, the two Sherman's Deli locations, the two El Mexicali Cafe locations). Second, structured-data scarcity is near-universal: of the 80 venues assessed, only a handful carry credible schema.org Restaurant markup with hours, menus, and addresses in machine-readable form. The rest either present clean human-readable content with no JSON-LD (Workshop Kitchen + Bar, Cheeky's, Bar Cecil, Le Vallauris, Wally's Desert Turtle, Sherman's), block automated fetchers entirely (Birba, Vicky's of Santa Fe, Castelli's, Mr. Lyons, theedgesteakhouse.com, mrlyonsf10.com), or have no functioning first-party domain at all (Arriola's, El Tranvia). Third, editorial weight skews toward Palm Springs and Palm Desert: East Valley heritage venues with multi-decade continuous family ownership — Arriola's (1927), Rincón Norteño (1964), El Tranvia (Coachella storefront ~15 years, family lineage 1969), Jalisco (1980), El Mexicali Cafe (1981) — are systematically under-described in valley-wide agent answers despite being older than most of the Palm Springs anchors that dominate the results.

This report maps where the dining identity actually sits, where the visibility gaps cluster, and where 'invisible but deserving' venues are being misread by agents reading a stale corpus.
input.relationships_narrative:
The competitive structure of Coachella Valley dining runs along three intersecting axes — corridor proximity, cuisine category, and credential tier — and these axes determine which venues win which queries. On the corridor axis, El Paseo and downtown Palm Springs are the two corridors where dining identity is most editorially contested. El Paseo's fine-dining cluster (Mastro's, Eddie V's, Sullivan's, Morton's, Ruth's Chris, Pacifica, Mitch's, Mamma Gina, Daily Grill) competes against itself for 'best fine dining Palm Desert' queries, with the chains carrying credential and structured-data advantages and Mitch's serving as the independent counterweight. Downtown Palm Springs is more fragmented: Le Vallauris, Spencer's, Melvyn's, Johnny Costa's, and Copley's hold the heritage tier; Bar Cecil, 4 Saints, Tac/Quila, and Workshop Kitchen + Bar hold the modern destination tier; the Tropicale, Mr. Lyons, and the Purple Room compete for the supper-club identity; Sherman's and Cheeky's anchor the deli and brunch categories. Rancho Mirage's Highway 111 corridor and its resort cluster compete in parallel — Wally's Desert Turtle holds the date-night anchor query against The Edge Steakhouse, Catalan, and Willie's Modern Fare on the corridor, while The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente holds the credential anchor against The Edge inside the resort tier.

On the cuisine axis, the most visible competition is in Italian, where Johnny Costa's, Castelli's, Mamma Gina, Birba, Bar Issi, Pinzimini, Double Date, and Ciro's all compete for editorial coverage that surfaces mostly in city-specific or corridor-specific contexts; no single Italian venue dominates the valley-wide 'best Italian Coachella Valley' query, and the Palm Springs Life Readers' Choice Best Italian streak (held by Johnny Costa's for a reported 13–15 consecutive years) is the most-cited credential. French cuisine concentrates in Palm Desert and La Quinta with Le Vallauris as the Palm Springs anchor; Cuistot's 2025 closure leaves a vacated French anchor on El Paseo. Mexican is fragmented along east-west lines: heritage in Indio and Coachella, elevated and modern in Palm Springs (Tac/Quila, Maleza, Clandestino). Sushi and Japanese are dominated by Nobu (Indian Wells) and Sandfish (Palm Springs), with Okura's 21-year La Quinta tenure largely invisible to editorial roundups. Steakhouses split along independent-versus-chain lines: The Edge, The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente, and Mr. Lyons on the independent side; Mastro's, Sullivan's, Morton's, Eddie V's, and Ruth's Chris on the chain side.

On the credential axis, the valley's only Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star restaurant is The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente, which it owns alongside an 18-year Wine Spectator Award of Excellence streak. AAA Four Diamond credentials are held by Wally's Desert Turtle (since 1993 per the restaurant, continuity not independently confirmed), The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente, and historically by Le Vallauris and Morgan's. Michelin Plate recognition for the 2024 and 2025 California guides is held by Bar Cecil, Workshop Kitchen + Bar, Tac/Quila, and Cheeky's, with Sandfish and 4 Saints also appearing as Michelin Recommended in those guides. James Beard Foundation recognition at the restaurant level is held by Workshop Kitchen + Bar (Outstanding Restaurant Design for SOMA Architects in 2015). DiRoNA membership is claimed by Wally's Desert Turtle, Chez Pierre, and La Quinta Cliffhouse, though current-year status was not independently verified against DiRoNA's roster. USA Today national recognition is held by El Mexicali Cafe II (Restaurants of the Year 2026) and Rooster and the Pig (Top 47 Restaurants 2025, often misframed as a 2026 win). National recognition is the credential tier where editorial coverage and agent surfacing diverge most sharply from review-volume and structured-data signals — venues with national press but weak schema (Rooster and the Pig, El Mexicali Cafe II) under-surface relative to their credentials, while venues with strong schema but no national press over-surface relative to theirs.
input.title: The Coachella Valley Dining Map: Corridors, Categories, and Agent-Visibility Gaps
